tool_name: OEE & TPM Analysis — Availability, Performance, Quality & Six Big Loss Breakdown
formulas: OT_h = planned_production_time_h - downtime_unplanned_h - downtime_setup_h; A = OT_h / planned_production_time_h   // [h, dimensionless] Operating Time & Availability (A) Reference: ISO 22400-2:2014 §4.3.3 — Availability = Operating Time / Planned Production Time
P = (total_parts_produced * ideal_cycle_time_s / 3600) / OT_h   // [dimensionless] Performance Rate (P) Reference: ISO 22400-2 §4.3.4 — Performance = (Total Pieces × Ideal CT) / Operating Time

good_parts = total_parts_produced - rejected_parts - rework_parts; Q = good_parts / total_parts_produced   // [dimensionless, parts] Quality Rate (Q) Reference: ISO 22400-2 §4.3.5 — Quality = Good Parts / Total Parts; rework excluded from good count
OEE = A * P * Q   // [dimensionless] OEE Score Reference: OEE = A × P × Q — JIPM TPM standard; World Class benchmark: OEE ≥ 85%

WC_OEE = 0.85; actual_good = good_parts; wc_good = Math.round(total_parts_produced * WC_OEE / OEE); parts_lost = wc_good - actual_good   // [parts] Throughput Loss (Good Parts Lost vs World-Class) Reference: Gap to World Class benchmark (85% OEE per JIPM) — production loss quantification

calendar_hours = 24 * Math.ceil(planned_production_time_h / 8); utilisation = planned_production_time_h / calendar_hours; TEEP = OEE * utilisation   // [dimensionless] TEEP (Total Effective Equipment Performance) Reference: TEEP = OEE × Utilisation — SEMI E10-0304; full-time calendar basis

V1.error_msg: Total downtime exceeds planned production time — check hours input

V2.error_msg: Defect + rework count exceeds total production — verify part counts

V3.error_msg: Performance rate > 105%: physically implausible — verify ideal cycle time; it may be set too conservative

smart_warnings.message: OEE < 65%: below minimum acceptable threshold — initiate full Six Big Loss analysis; priority losses: breakdowns (Loss 1) and setup time (Loss 2)
Availability < 80%: unplanned downtime dominant loss — implement Planned Maintenance (PM Step 3) and predictive maintenance programme
Quality rate < 95%: >5% defect/rework — conduct root cause analysis per 8D; check tooling wear schedule and incoming material certification
Performance < 85%: speed loss and minor stoppages — install cycle time monitoring; target micro-stoppage elimination through Autonomous Maintenance (AM Step 2)
TEEP < 50%: significant unused capacity — evaluate shift extension or weekend running before CAPEX investment per JIPM TPM §6
